currentState.routeSummary: Keep the problem centered on the exponential-growth lower-bound route implied by distinct subset sums.
currentState.nextHonestMove: Record the first reduction and checkpoint it without overclaiming theorem progress.

granularBreakdownMode.label: ORP Granular Breakdown
granularBreakdownMode.activationPhrase: Break it down until it can move.
granularBreakdownMode.invocationRule: Use the full breakdown before executing a complex theorem/search packet; use the nudge card whenever the agent or user is confused, the target feels too broad, or the next action is not deterministic.

commands.fullBreakdown: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 1 | State Claim Honestly" --json

loopIntegration: Run the full breakdown after the theorem/search surfaces are audited and before executing the current work packet.
Write operationally important decompositions into TASK_LIST, FORMALIZATION_WORK, or a dedicated checklist artifact rather than leaving them in chat.
Use the nudge card for quick reorientation when a step becomes blurry during implementation.
Compress the breakdown back into one active target and one verification command before continuing.
granularBreakdownMode.durableArtifactRule: If the breakdown changes what we should do, refresh the canonical task list so the new dependency ladder becomes repo-owned state.

orpModeOverlays.selectionRule: Select the smallest ORP overlay that reduces ambiguity or friction; do not run every mode by default.

overlays.label: Granular Breakdown
overlays.useWhen: The work is broad, confusing, theorem-heavy, or needs a durable dependency ladder before execution.
overlays.command: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 1 | State Claim Honestly" --json
overlays.loopPlacement: Before execute_current_work_packet and whenever the active target becomes fuzzy.
overlays.outputExpectation: Boundary, lanes, subclaims, atomic obligations, dependency ladder, active target, durable checklist, and next verification.

overlays.label: Ruthless Simplification
overlays.useWhen: A plan, proof surface, or final response is swollen, repetitive, or hiding the single live decision.
overlays.loopPlacement: After granular decomposition, before committing a plan to TASK_LIST, and before final summaries.
overlays.outputExpectation: One core sentence, one surviving move, and a list of noise to delete or defer.

overlays.label: Systems Constellation
overlays.useWhen: A change touches multiple surfaces such as erdos-problems, ORP, frontier-engine, local/remote compute, task lists, or user workflow.
overlays.loopPlacement: Before cross-system integration, paid/remote compute decisions, or changes to canonical source/writeback flow.
overlays.outputExpectation: Upstreams, downstreams, feedback loops, hidden costs, and the system-level decision.

overlays.label: Bold Concept Generation
overlays.useWhen: The active theorem/search lane has converged too early, needs new candidate lemmas, or a split atom fails and needs alternative constructions.
overlays.loopPlacement: After a falsifier, before launching broad compute, or when inventing the next symbolic construction.
overlays.outputExpectation: Several meaningfully different candidate moves, with the boldest one grounded into a quick experiment.

overlays.label: Sleek Minimal Progressive
overlays.useWhen: The work feels flat, overly linear, or technically correct but hard for future collaborators to pick up.
overlays.loopPlacement: When refining docs, task-list readability, handoff artifacts, UI surfaces, or the shape of the next user-facing explanation.
overlays.outputExpectation: One subtraction, one tasteful surprise, and one concrete next move that keeps momentum.

agentFlow.purpose: Give agents one compact execution contract without forcing humans or agents through every task-list section.
agentFlow.operatingRule: Read this object first; execute the primary next action unless a guardrail blocks it or the task list is stale.

primaryNextAction.task: state_claim_honestly: Write a claim statement that is no stronger than the active claim-pass support surface.

modePolicy.selectionRule: Select the smallest ORP overlay that reduces ambiguity or friction; do not run every mode by default.

granularBreakdown.useWhen: Use when the primary action is broad, fuzzy, theorem-heavy, or has unclear dependencies.
granularBreakdown.fullBreakdownCommand: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 1 | State Claim Honestly" --json
granularBreakdown.compressionRule: After any breakdown, compress back to one active target and one verification command.

situationalOverlays.useWhen: A plan, proof surface, or final response is swollen, repetitive, or hiding the single live decision.
situationalOverlays.loopPlacement: After granular decomposition, before committing a plan to TASK_LIST, and before final summaries.
situationalOverlays.outputExpectation: One core sentence, one surviving move, and a list of noise to delete or defer.

situationalOverlays.useWhen: A change touches multiple surfaces such as erdos-problems, ORP, frontier-engine, local/remote compute, task lists, or user workflow.
situationalOverlays.loopPlacement: Before cross-system integration, paid/remote compute decisions, or changes to canonical source/writeback flow.
situationalOverlays.outputExpectation: Upstreams, downstreams, feedback loops, hidden costs, and the system-level decision.

situationalOverlays.useWhen: The active theorem/search lane has converged too early, needs new candidate lemmas, or a split atom fails and needs alternative constructions.
situationalOverlays.loopPlacement: After a falsifier, before launching broad compute, or when inventing the next symbolic construction.
situationalOverlays.outputExpectation: Several meaningfully different candidate moves, with the boldest one grounded into a quick experiment.

situationalOverlays.useWhen: The work feels flat, overly linear, or technically correct but hard for future collaborators to pick up.
situationalOverlays.loopPlacement: When refining docs, task-list readability, handoff artifacts, UI surfaces, or the shape of the next user-facing explanation.
situationalOverlays.outputExpectation: One subtraction, one tasteful surprise, and one concrete next move that keeps momentum.

executionLoop: If the action is clear, execute its command or implement the named task directly.
If the action is fuzzy, run the granular nudge or full breakdown, then compress to one target and one command.
Use at most one additional ORP overlay when it clearly reduces friction for the current decision.
Refresh the canonical task list after material progress and continue from the regenerated agentFlow.
guardrails: Do not run paid or remote compute unless the frontier configuration explicitly enables that paid rung.
Prefer local/free compute before opt-in paid compute.
Do not treat bounded search evidence as an all-N proof without a theorem-facing handoff.
Do not run every ORP mode by default.
Persist operationally important changes into canonical artifacts rather than chat-only state.

writeback.durableArtifactRule: If the breakdown changes what we should do, refresh the canonical task list so the new dependency ladder becomes repo-owned state.

executionRule.summary: Treat the task list as a concrete research loop for problem 1: execute the highest-value task itself, not wrapper prose about the task.
afterEachCompletion: Re-read the theorem loop, claim pass, formalization packet, formalization work packet, and verifier boundary.
Use ORP granular breakdown when the next step is broad or confusing: orp mode nudge granular-breakdown --json

Use the smallest relevant ORP mode overlay when it simplifies the work: simplification for bloat, systems for cross-surface consequences, bold generation for new candidates, and sleek-progressive for clearer handoff shape.
Invent the next highest-value step from the updated evidence rather than following a stale fixed script.
Append that next step to the task list and refresh the canonical task-list artifacts.

granularBreakdownMode.fullBreakdownCommand: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 1 | State Claim Honestly" --json

executionRule.repetitionProtocol: To execute this loop 10 times or 100 times, complete the first ready task, refresh the task list, then use the regenerated ordering for the next pass.
executionRule.lowStressLongHorizonNote: This loop is meant to be safe for long-horizon execution because it re-ranks after every pass instead of committing to a blind fixed plan.

loopTemplate.title: Freeze the current north star and active theorem packet
loopTemplate.task: Freeze the current route `distinct_subset_sum_lower_bound`, claim surface `route_scaffolding`, and active theorem packet before widening the search.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The theorem loop, claim pass, and formalization packet all agree on the current route, next honest move, and active target.

loopTemplate.title: Audit theorem, claim, and formalization surfaces for consistency
loopTemplate.task: Audit the theorem loop, claim loop, claim pass, formalization packet, and formalization work packet for drift or contradictory wording.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The canonical theorem/search surfaces tell one consistent story about what is supported, what is open, and what the active work unit is.

loopTemplate.title: Verify canonical pack inputs against the latest route and verifier artifacts
loopTemplate.task: Verify that the pack-backed theorem surfaces still match the latest route packet, OPS details, and verifier-facing artifacts.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The canonical input packet for this loop remains current and trustworthy.

loopTemplate.title: Verify the current highest-value structural hook remains canonical
loopTemplate.task: Verify that the strongest supported structural hook still survives across the theorem and claim surfaces.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The primary theorem-facing hook still survives refresh and remains narrow enough to formalize honestly.

loopTemplate.title: Verify the current verifier boundary and next target
loopTemplate.task: Verify that the current verifier boundary and next target are stated honestly across the pack surfaces.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The verifier-facing boundary remains explicit even without a dedicated interval queue.

loopTemplate.title: Apply ORP granular breakdown before execution
loopTemplate.task: Run ORP granular breakdown on the active topic `problem 1 | State Claim Honestly` and compress it into one active target plus one verification command.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The broad work packet has been decomposed into boundary, lanes, atomic obligations, dependency ladder, active target, durable checklist, and next verification.
loopTemplate.command: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 1 | State Claim Honestly" --json

loopTemplate.title: State Claim Honestly
loopTemplate.task: State the candidate theorem-facing claim in pack language without overstating current support.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The first remaining gap is either discharged, narrowed, or replaced by a sharper one: state_claim_honestly: Write a claim statement that is no stronger than the active claim-pass support surface.

loopTemplate.title: Refresh the task list and rerank the next step
loopTemplate.task: Refresh the canonical task list after the completed work so the next iteration is driven by the newest theorem/search/verifier evidence.
loopTemplate.completionRule: The refreshed task list records the next highest-value action instead of relying on a stale fixed script.

nextNeededSteps.task: state_claim_honestly: Write a claim statement that is no stronger than the active claim-pass support surface.
freeze_support_artifacts: Attach theorem-loop, claim-pass, and any bridge-backed packet paths directly to the packet.
define_promotion_boundary: Record explicit promotion criteria tied to current falsifiers.
Record the first reduction and checkpoint it without overclaiming theorem progress.
